differential_diagnoses: Primary pleural lymphoma (or pulmonary involvement of occult lymphoma)
Metastatic carcinoma (e.g., lung, breast, or ovary)
next_steps: Perform flow cytometry on pleural fluid for surface marker analysis
Send pleural fluid for cytology and cell block preparation
Request pleural fluid ADA and QuantiFERON-TB Gold testing
Obtain CT scan of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast
Perform video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery (VATS) with pleural biopsy
Measure serum protein electrophoresis with free light chains and immunofixation

differential_diagnoses: Primary pulmonary or pleural lymphoma (e.g., MALT lymphoma)
Metastatic adenocarcinoma (e.g., lung, ovary, or breast)
Plasma-cell neoplasm (e.g., plasmacytoma or multiple myeloma)
next_steps: Perform pleural fluid cytology, flow cytometry, and cell block analysis
Obtain CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast
Order serum protein electrophoresis (SPEP) and immunofixation with serum free light chains
Measure pleural fluid pH, glucose, ADA, and fungal cultures
Perform video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery (VATS) with pleural biopsy for definitive histology
Measure serum CA-125 and CEA levels
